parsed.rationale: The Caudal fin is located at the posterior end of the fish skeleton. Visually, the rays extending from the Caudal fin are significantly longer and more numerous than those of the Dorsal, Anal, or Pelvic fins. The other fins are positioned along the body and have shorter, more compact ray structures. Therefore, the Caudal fin has the longest fin rays.
